text: On Monday morning, Priya arrived at the office thirty minutes early.
She did not want to seem awkward on her first day.
A colleague named Sam showed her around and explained how things worked.
The pace was overwhelming at first — so many names, so many rules.
Priya felt keen to do well, but she worried about making mistakes.
On Wednesday she missed a deadline because she had not understood the task properly.
Her manager did not seem angry, though.
Instead, he offered clear guidance and told her that the outcome of the first week did not define her future at the company.
She realised that no one expected her to impress everyone straight away.
Settling in takes time, and asking for help is not a weakness — it is a sign of good judgement.
